title: aglaia luzoniensis
intro: Aglaia luzoniensis, also known as Luzon aglaia, is a species of plant in the family Meliaceae, native to the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating respiratory issues like bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, Aglaia luzoniensis is often used in traditional medicine, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which may be taken internally or applied topically to alleviate symptoms.
